title: Panel – Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research? – International Conference on Social Media & Society
snippet: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a panelist in the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' at the SMSociety 2026 conference, discussing experiences and frustrations with the Meta Content Library.
description: Fabio Giglietto is listed as a panelist for the 'Meta Content Library Community Roundtable: Where to for Social Media Research?' at the SMSociety 2026 conference, indicating his involvement in discussions directly related to his expertise.

title: How Facebook's Algorithmic Tweaks Affected Engagement with News URLs over Time | Snurblog — Axel Bruns
snippet: This blog post by Axel Bruns reports on Fabio Giglietto's presentation at the 2026 International Communication Association conference, focusing on his research about partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic amplification on Facebook. The article details Giglietto's study on how URLs shared on Facebook reach audiences of varying sizes, considering the impact of partisanship, news quality, and Facebook's algorithmic governance changes over time.
description: This blog post by Axel Bruns reports on Fabio Giglietto's 2026 International Communication Association conference presentation, detailing his research on partisan alignment, journalistic quality, and algorithmic amplification on Facebook.

title: Secondo uno studio dell'università di Urbino l'algoritmo di Facebook ora punisce la politica ufficiale e premia gli estremisti antieuropei. Pd e FdI verso una legge bipartisan sulle piattaforme - Open
snippet: This news article reports on a study published on January 7, 2026, by the University of Urbino, authored by Professor Fabio Giglietto, which concludes that Facebook's algorithm is penalizing institutional politics and favoring anti-European extremists, filorussian, and conspiracy content.
description: This news article reports on a recent study by Professor Fabio Giglietto of the University of Urbino, analyzing Facebook's algorithm and its impact on institutional politics versus extremist content.
